comments.author: @bradleyyoung4142
comments.text: We left Louisiana moved to Colorado springs and even though you need 1/4 million dollars to buy a house inside the springs it's the best place to live in usa. Now you can get a nice piece of property for better prices down by Pueblo,  and get raw land for 5 grand down by walsenburg. Just an fyi don't waist your time in or around Denver, it's suffering from the same thing as every other American city. Just a dumpster 🔥 fire

comments.author: @John-vp2jq
comments.text: The mountains are a key component of why Colorado is 1A on my personal ranking of states.  A variety of industries thrive along the Front Range, allowing for abundant economic opportunity, while the State's incredible nature are just a hop, skip and a jump away - often literally!  I once lived in an area of Boulder where you could roll out of bed, throw on some clothes and be on trail without even realizing it.

comments.author: @BrendtOConnell
comments.text: Thank you. I'm rocky mountain lucky

comments.author: @prototropo
comments.text: Budweiswer is hardly a craft brewery. You could have listed 30 local, and far better, brews. Golden, Boulder, Pueblo, Durango, Breckenridge, Aspen, Greeley and Fort Collins all have great microbrews.

replies.author: @rockchalk966
replies.text: This amazing video and you still find this to complain about ? I could watch this video on loop and I’ve been in Colorado my whole life

comments.author: @prototropo
comments.text: Gorgeous survey of my state. Colorado was even more sublimely beautiful in the 1950s—before suburban sprawl marred the great Front Range landscapes. I wish development was regulated! We need vertical in-fill, not horizontal spread-out.

replies.author: @2eyedhairlessmnkey
replies.text: Are you saying more high rises? I'd almost rather urban sprawl.

replies.author: @2eyedhairlessmnkey
replies.text: I feel it's my state too. Since 1972 born on the West slope. It's hard to see the mountain towns get gentrified as well. But what do you say, it's progress...

comments.author: @alvinlee10ya
comments.text: What's messed up is that you all didn't even give a slight mention of Breckenridge Brewery in Breckenridge , but you flash a big scene of a Budweiser Brewery in Fort Collins !

comments.author: @SparkyFromColorado
comments.text: One minor problem. While you're discussing the Durango & Silverton, the shot from 13:10 to 13:18 shows the Georgetown Loop Railroad, not the Durango & Silverton Narrow Gauge Railroad. The two are on opposite sides of the Rockies, basically.

comments.text: Colorado isn’t just mountains , it’s geology on steroids. From alpine tundra to desert dunes in one state is crazy.
